name: Canakinumab had an exploratory lung-cancer signal in CANTOS
summary: The CANTOS cancer analysis suggests possible IL-1beta relevance beyond cardiovascular disease, but it is hypothesis-generating.
statement: In an exploratory CANTOS analysis, canakinumab was associated with dose-dependent reductions in incident lung cancer and lung-cancer mortality, alongside the same infection-risk caveat.
population_or_model: CANTOS participants with atherosclerosis and no previously diagnosed cancer
quantitative_note: Incident lung cancer hazard ratios were 0.61 for 150 mg and 0.33 for 300 mg versus placebo; lung-cancer mortality was lower in the 300 mg group with HR 0.23.
caveats: The analysis was exploratory and requires replication in formal cancer prevention or treatment settings.
The population had atherosclerosis and residual inflammation, not a general aging cohort.
Fatal infections or sepsis were significantly more common in the canakinumab groups.
